name: TruthMirror
description: This codebase is an ongoing multi agent geopolitical intelligence and fact-analysis engine. It takes a claim, and uses LLMs breaks it into smaller sub-claims, gathers live evidence from multiple sources, tags the evidence by perspective, and then uses AI to synthesize the results into a structured verdict and narrative summary. Instead of giving only a simple true/false answer, it aims to show the broader context, disputes, and source framing around the claim. (Under Development as of June 2026)
tech: Wikipedia, arxiv, Crossref etc APIs/Web Scraping

name: EmailSpamClassifier
description: This project is a Python machine learning pipeline for classifying SMS messages as Spam or Ham (not spam). It automatically downloads the SMS Spam Collection dataset, preprocesses the text, converts messages into TF-IDF features, trains a Multinomial Naive Bayes model, and evaluates its performance with accuracy and a classification report. The model achieves around 97% accuracy on the test set.

name: DigitRecognizer
description: This is a deep learning project for handwritten digit recognition using the MNIST dataset. It builds and trains a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to classify grayscale images of digits from 0 to 9, reaching about 99% test accuracy. The pipeline includes data loading, preprocessing, augmentation, model training, evaluation, visualization, and saving the trained model for later use

name: ChessAgainstAI
description: This is simple a web-based chess game (made for learning) where a player can play against an AI opponent in the browser. The project is mainly a learning-focused application that combines game logic, user interaction, and a simple server into one working app.
